primary_accounting_issue: Whether an entity can apply hedge accounting documentation in consolidated financial statements to hedge foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend receivable

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 6.3.5 prohibits hedge accounting for intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements; paragraph 6.3.6 provides the exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items where FX gains/losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation

supporting_authority.reason: Governs recognition of foreign exchange differences on monetary items, which determines whether FX exposure is eliminated on consolidation for purposes of the paragraph 6.3.6 exception

Clarifies that hedge accounting for foreign currency risk can only be applied to the FX exposure between functional currencies and addresses how net investment hedges work in consolidated financial statements

peripheral_authority.reason: General hedge accounting framework for hedged items and hedging instruments, but does not specifically address the intragroup transaction restriction or exception
Disclosures for liabilities arising from financing activities, not directly relevant to hedge documentation eligibility for intragroup dividends

treatment_families.family: Eligibility for hedge accounting on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: General prohibition on intragroup hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5, hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements cannot be applied to transactions between entities in the same group, including intragroup dividend receivables, unless the specific exception applies

approaches.label: Exception for intragroup FX monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6, foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements if the FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation, which applies when entities have different functional currencies
